stories.title: Ryan Fox Wins The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RYAN FOX WINS THE 2026 OPEN CHAMPIONSHIP. 🏆

200-1 odds. World No. 56. Age 39. First major.

Birdie on 18 from 12 feet. Claret Jug.

The most unlikely major winner in years.

#TheOpen #RoyalBirkdale #Golf

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Ryan Fox: 39 years old. World No. 56. 200-1 longshot.

He birdied 4 of his last 6 holes at Royal Birkdale.

He sank a 12-foot birdie putt on 18 to win The Open by one shot.

Only the third New Zealander to win the Claret Jug.

#TheOpen #Golf

article.headline: Ryan Fox Sinks the Putt on 18 to Win the 2026 Open Championship

stories.title: Sam Burns' Collapse — History Not Seen in 29 Years

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Sam Burns entered Sunday's round with a 2-shot lead.

He birdied hole 2 to go to -11.

Then: bogey, bogey, bogey (holes 4-5-6).

He parred his final 12 holes. Shot 72. Finished -8.

Ryan Fox shot 68 and walked away with the Claret Jug.

#TheOpen #Golf #SamBurns

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Sam Burns' Open collapse: 54-hole leader squandering the lead at The Open in a way not seen in 29 years.

He had the tournament.

Holes 4, 5, and 6 on Sunday took it away.

That's golf. That's why we watch.

#TheOpen #Golf

article.headline: Sam Burns Had the Open Championship. Three Holes on Sunday Took It Away.

stories.title: Cameron Young's Sunday 64 — The Runner-Up That Won't Be Forgotten

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Cameron Young shot 64 on Sunday at Royal Birkdale.

He briefly held the lead. He played nearly perfect golf.

One shot. Fox's birdie on 18.

Runner-up. But what a round.

#TheOpen #CameronYoung #Golf

article.headline: Cameron Young Shot 64 on Sunday. Ryan Fox Shot 68. One Putt Separated Them.

research.facts:
Cameron Young's Sunday 64
**Source 1:** Golf Channel / PGA Tour — Young shot 9-under final round; actually the source says "64" for final round
**Source 2:** Yahoo Sports — confirmed Young finished -9, one back of Fox
**Note:** Research says Young shot "a stunning 64 on Sunday" — confirmed across Golf Channel, PGA Tour, Yahoo Sports
**Verified facts:**
- Young final round: 64 (HIGH)
- Young final score: -9 (HIGH)
- Young briefly held the lead as Burns faltered (HIGH — PGA Tour live blog)
- Fox's 18th hole birdie won it by one over Young (HIGH)

stories.title: LIV Golf Funding Crisis — Saudi PIF Ending Backing After 2026

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Saudi Arabia is pulling funding from LIV Golf after 2026.

$6 billion invested. Annual losses of ~$500M.

The CEO can't guarantee the final 4 events will happen.

LIV is looking for new investors. Time is running out.

#LIVGolf #Golf

article.headline: Saudi Arabia Is Ending LIV Golf Funding. The Final Four Events May Be at Risk.

research.facts:
LIV Golf Funding Crisis
**Source 1:** CNBC (April 2026) — PIF confirms ending funding after 2026 season; $6B+ invested since 2022; $500-600M annual losses
**Source 2:** CBS Sports — PIF funding ending; 4 events remain in 2026
**Source 3:** Golf Channel — LIV CEO Scott O'Neil could not guarantee remaining events; independent board formed (Gene Davis, Jon Zinman)
**Source 4:** Front Office Sports — PIF "may cut payments before season ends" (per paddock insiders); final four events at risk
**Source 5:** Golf.com — Ducera Partners hired for financial restructuring
**Verified facts:**
- PIF ends backing after 2026 (HIGH — CNBC April 2026 announcement)
- $6B+ invested since 2022 (HIGH — multiple confirm)
- Annual losses ~$500-600M (HIGH)
- 4 events remain in 2026 (HIGH)
- CEO O'Neil could not guarantee events (HIGH — Golf Channel)
- PIF may cut before season ends (MEDIUM — insiders per Front Office Sports)
- Independent board: Gene Davis, Jon Zinman (HIGH)
- Ducera Partners hired (HIGH — Golf.com)

stories.title: McIlroy's Open — Not the Result He Wanted After Masters Glory

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy won the 2026 Masters in April.

At The Open? He made the cut at -1. Never in contention.

His last Open win was Hoylake in 2014.

The major to complete his collection still waits.

#Rory #TheOpen #Golf

article.headline: McIlroy's Open Week: Masters Glory in April, Birkdale Frustration in July

research.facts:
McIlroy at The Open
**Source 1:** PGA Tour live blog — McIlroy R1: 2-over 72, tied ~114th; R2: 67, made cut at -1
**Source 2:** CBS Sports — McIlroy criticized DeChambeau's penalty reaction as "performative" and "for attention"
**Source 3:** Yahoo Sports — McIlroy last Open win: Hoylake 2014; chasing 7th major; won 2026 Masters (second Green Jacket, back-to-back with 2025)
**Source 4:** Research notes — McIlroy won Scottish Open before The Open; "chasing 7th major"
**Verified facts:**
- McIlroy won 2026 Masters (HIGH)
- McIlroy R1: 2-over 72 (HIGH)
- McIlroy R2: 67, made cut at -1 (HIGH)
- McIlroy not in contention for final weekend (HIGH)
- McIlroy last Open win: Hoylake 2014 (HIGH)
- McIlroy criticized DeChambeau: "not particularly fond... performative... for attention" (HIGH — CBS Sports)
- DeChambeau R2 penalty: Rule 8.1a (stepped on fescue near ball, improved swing area) — dropped from -7 to -5 (HIGH — from 07-19 pipeline research)
